headline: Google I/O 2026: Gemini 3.5 Flash GA — Outperforms Previous Pro on Agents; Gemini Omni Launches
summary: Google released Gemini 3.5 Flash (generally available) at I/O 2026, beating Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic benchmarks (Terminal-Bench 2.1: 76.2%, MCP Atlas: 83.6%), available via Antigravity, AI Studio, and Android Studio. Gemini Omni is a new multimodal series accepting image/audio/video/text and outputting video grounded in real-world knowledge. Gemini 3.5 Pro is in testing with release expected next month.
foryou: Flash outperforming the previous Pro on agentic benchmarks at lower cost is a significant shift — re-benchmark your current model routing logic now; Flash likely captures most of your Claude/GPT-5.5 use cases at meaningfully lower cost.

specs.label: Availability
specs.value: GA — Antigravity, AI Studio, Android Studio

headline: Anthropic Claude Mythos Preview: 10,000+ Critical Bugs Found Across 50 Partner Organizations
summary: Anthropic's Project Glasswing published results from ~50 partner organizations deploying Claude Mythos Preview in defensive security workflows: over 10,000 high/critical-severity vulnerabilities identified in widely-used software. General release is conditioned on stronger safeguards — not expected before late 2026 or 2027. Claude Security, using Opus 4.7 for code scanning + fix generation, launched in public beta May 22 for Enterprise customers.
foryou: Mythos Preview's bug-finding rate is a wake-up call for any deployed software — enrolling in the Glasswing enterprise program before general release gives you a meaningful security advantage that disappears once it's available to everyone.

headline: GPT-5.5 Instant Is Now the Default ChatGPT Model as OpenAI Ramps Toward September IPO
summary: OpenAI made GPT-5.5 Instant the default model for all ChatGPT users, replacing GPT-5.5 which launched April 23. The move reflects confidence in GPT-5.5 Instant's cost-quality tradeoff ahead of OpenAI's planned September IPO. Amazon is reportedly in talks to invest $10B, and Novo Nordisk announced a strategic AI partnership covering drug discovery, clinical trials, and manufacturing.
foryou: Default model shifts at this scale represent OpenAI's cost-quality confidence signal — re-run your API benchmarks against GPT-5.5 Instant to update your routing logic before it affects your production baseline.

headline: OpenAI Confidentially Files S-1, Targets $852B–$1T Valuation in September IPO
summary: OpenAI filed its S-1 confidentially with the SEC on May 22, targeting a September 2026 listing at $852B–$1T valuation. Revenue grew from $6B in 2024 to $25B annualized by February 2026, but the company projects a $14B loss in 2026 — losing $1.22 per dollar of revenue in Q1. Profitability not expected until ~2030. CFO Sarah Friar has expressed reservations about timing.

headline: Anthropic's Multi-Supplier Compute Strategy: Google $40B + Broadcom Multi-GW Deal on Top of SpaceX $45B
summary: Google announced an investment of up to $40B in Anthropic (cash + compute, April 24) while a separate Broadcom partnership adds custom silicon capacity at multiple-gigawatt scale. Combined with the SpaceX $45B deal and Microsoft Maia talks, Anthropic has built the most diversified compute supply chain of any AI lab — no single vendor dependency.

headline: US Federal AI Spending Surges 966% to $7.2B Obligated in FY2026; GSA Proposes First AI Clause
summary: Federal AI obligations reached $7.2B in FY2026 (up 966% from 2024) with potential awards totaling $91.8B (up 1,912%). The GSA proposed GSAR clause 552.239-7001 covering government data ownership, prohibitions on using government data to train AI, and disclosure obligations. Multiple states also enacted broad AI governance statutes with enforcement beginning in 2026.

headline: Anthropic Introduces 'Dreaming': AI Agents Autonomously Review Prior Sessions to Self-Improve
summary: Anthropic's 'dreaming' technique allows AI agents to review their own behavior between sessions, identify patterns, and self-improve — targeting long-running coding, finance, and legal workflows. Unlike standard RLHF, dreaming runs autonomously without human rating labels, using the agent's own trajectory as the improvement signal.
foryou: Session-to-session self-improvement without human labels is a step-change for any long-running autonomous agent — directly applicable to coding agents or data pipeline agents you're already running in production.

headline: Cloudflare + Stripe Enable AI Agents to Open Accounts, Buy Domains, and Deploy Apps Autonomously
summary: Cloudflare and Stripe introduced a standardized protocol allowing AI agents to create accounts, purchase domains, and deploy applications without human intervention — covering standardized identity, authorization, and payment flows. Compatible with any AI framework and live on Cloudflare Workers.
foryou: Autonomous agent transactions are now infrastructure-layer supported — prototyping a buy-and-deploy pipeline is feasible today, and the range of fully-automatable workflows just expanded dramatically.
